# Kungfu Kanban

> A local-first kanban board for macOS where every card runs a Claude Code
> agent on your existing Claude subscription — no API keys, no token billing,
> no cloud. Cards create, route, run, and review themselves; repo cards open
> real GitHub pull requests. Free, MIT-licensed open source.

Key facts:

- Runs entirely on your machine: one Express server, plain JSON files in
  `data/`; no accounts, no telemetry from the board app.
- Uses the Claude Code CLI with your subscription login; the runner strips
  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from the environment so it can never bill per token.
- An LLM manager (the "Sensei") triages cards — model, effort, priority,
  skills — dispatches runs, and reviews results against acceptance criteria
  and the actual PR diff, with three autonomy levels (suggest / semi / auto).
  It can merge a card's PR itself once CI is green, send precise same-session
  follow-up fixes instead of always restarting, and chain multi-card plans
  with dependencies.
- Repo cards run in isolated git worktrees and open pull requests via the
  GitHub CLI, gated by CI; merged PRs file themselves into Done; conflicted
  PRs spawn self-healing fix agents.
- Cards can depend on each other ("runs after"): a dependent waits until its
  prerequisite is actually done — an open, unmerged PR still counts as
  blocking. Cards can also share a group, which clusters them under one
  header, runs one card per group at a time, and sends a single
  group-complete notification.
- Subscription rate limits are absorbed: cards requeue, a countdown shows, and
  everything resumes when the window resets. Model-specific caps step launches
  down the fable → opus → sonnet → haiku ladder automatically.
- Phone access via token gate + Tailscale serve (tailnet-only), installable as
  a PWA, with ntfy push notifications.
- Import work from pasted markdown, dropped `.md` files, or open GitHub
  issues; a bundled `kungfu-todo` skill lets any Claude Code session queue
  cards onto the board.
- Ships with the ponytail skill (vendored, MIT): agents default to the
  laziest working solution — less code, fewer output tokens per card, so a
  subscription rate-limit window goes further. Auto-installed at boot and
  pre-selected on new cards.

## Docs

- [The manual](https://kungfu-kanban.com/#manual): complete setup and feature
  reference — requirements, install, PR flow, the Manager, phone access,
  limits, security.
- [Docs](https://kungfu-kanban.com/docs): linkable reference sections — the
  markdown import format, the Sensei's actions and autonomy levels,
  dependency/merge-gate semantics, and groups & lanes.
- [Compare](https://kungfu-kanban.com/compare): an honest comparison against
  Vibe Kanban, Conductor, and Crystal/Nimbalyst — the other tools people
  evaluate for running coding agents.
- [README](https://github.com/LeahyCC/kungfu-kanban#readme): the same manual
  on GitHub.

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/LeahyCC/kungfu-kanban): MIT-licensed
  source code, issues, and releases.
